ATTY. DEXTER B. LOUIE OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative summary Adjudication- The motion for summary judgment is denied. The Bietz declaration, the objections to which are overruled, raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant's staff met the standard of care. The motion for summary adjudication as to the second cause of action for battery is granted. Plaintiff has conceded that restraining him does not constitute a battery by virtue of implied consent in the treatment of an emergency. Rather, plaintiff complains of the manner and duration of the restraint. That raises issues of negligence, but does not constitute such a different procedure as to support a claim for medical battery. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MALCOME LEADER-PICONE, ESQ., 510.444.2404, FOR PLAINTIFF ROY POOLE.
MARCELL PATRICK NERI, ESQ., 415.288.9800, FOR DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER)'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. is sustained with 10 DAYS leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled facts showing Defendant performed a type of treatment substantially different from that for which it had any necessary consent. Indeed, Plaintiff alleges continuing express consent to restraint, which is inconsistent with his argued, but unpleaded, contention of withdrawal of consent. Plaintiff would not now be permitted to allege that he did not ask that his wrist be re-tied, and his allegation that he suffered injury from the manner in which he was restrained during treatment of his seizure would constitute negligence, not battery. Plaintiff has not requested leave to amend or argued any facts that could be pleaded in good faith that could allow this claim to go forward. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
